mechanism_narrative: Sustained high unemployment above a natural rate threshold indicates significant slack in the economy. This slack suppresses wage growth and consumer demand, leading to lower corporate earnings expectations and negative pressure on equity prices over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is currently 8.5%, well above historical full-employment levels.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time when the trigger condition (UNRATE > 6.5%) is met over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A near-zero federal funds rate provides a powerful tailwind for equities by drastically lowering the discount rate on future cash flows and encouraging capital to move into risk assets (the 'TINA' effect). This mechanism is always active when rates are at the effective lower bound.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS is currently 0.07%, indicating a zero-interest-rate policy (ZIRP) is in effect.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return during periods where FEDFUNDS < 0.25% is not statistically different from periods where it is higher (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in real retail sales growth is a leading indicator of weakening consumer demand, which constitutes the largest part of the US economy. This directly impacts revenue forecasts for a majority of S&P 500 companies, leading to negative returns.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year growth in RSAFS minus year-over-year growth in CPIAUCSL is negative.
evidence_refs: fact:Nominal spending must be adjusted for inflation to gauge real demand.
failure_condition: The signal's hit rate for predicting negative 20-day returns is below 55% over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production reflects a robust manufacturing and supply side of the economy. This signals that companies are successfully meeting demand, leading to strong corporate profits and positive short-term equity performance.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between 3-month changes in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.03 over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Strong, accelerating growth in nonfarm payrolls is a coincident indicator of broad economic health. This strength confirms positive business conditions and robust consumer income, boosting investor sentiment and driving positive short-term returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The latest PAYEMS value is 131,900 thousands.
failure_condition: The average 5-day return following a trigger event is not statistically significantly greater than zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation rises while the central bank holds policy rates near zero, real interest rates become sharply negative. This condition erodes the value of fixed income assets and cash, forcing capital into inflation-hedging assets like equities, but also signals policy error, creating volatility. The net effect over the medium term is negative as fears of future aggressive tightening grow.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS is 0.07% and CPIAUCSL is 226.72, implying positive YoY inflation.
failure_condition: In a multivariate regression, the interaction term between CPIAUCSL growth and the level of FEDFUNDS does not have a statistically significant negative coefficient on 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: The housing market is a long-leading indicator for the broader economy. A sustained downturn in housing starts signals tightening financial conditions and weakening consumer confidence, presaging a broader economic slowdown and negatively impacting equity markets.
evidence_refs: fact:The current HOUST value of 685.0 is substantially below typical cycle peaks.
failure_condition: The signal does not show a negative Sharpe ratio over a full walk-forward test period covering at least one recession.

mechanism_narrative: When the economy is at or near full employment (low UNRATE), continued strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) signals potential overheating. This leads to wage-push inflation, compresses corporate margins, and invites monetary tightening, all of which are negative for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:This hypothesis is currently inactive as UNRATE is 8.5%.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances during the walk-forward validation period.
